title: Analyzing a Successful Web Task
concept: Tracing how an agent searched, navigated, extracted data, and submitted a query on a site.

exercises.prompt: What do you think is the first step in using a search engine?

options.text: Waiting for the results to appear

exercises.explain: The first step is to type your question into the search bar.

cards.title: What's a Web Task?
cards.body: A web task is any job or action you do online, like searching for information or filling out a form.

cards.title: What is an Agent?
cards.body: An agent is a program that can search, find, and use information on the web on your behalf.

cards.title: Agents Are Like Helpful Assistants
cards.body: Imagine an assistant who knows all the best websites; you just ask them, and they quickly find what you need!

cards.title: Example of a Web Task
cards.body: When you want to find recipes, an agent could search for 'chocolate cake recipes,' pick the best links, and show you the plans.

cards.title: Myth: Agents Do All the Work for You
cards.body: While agents help a lot, you still need to guide them with clear questions and check their findings.

cards.title: Searching the Web
cards.body: Let's look at how an agent performs a web task!

left.text: Typing a question in the search bar.

right.text: Viewing and selecting links from search results.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of an agent when performing a web search?
exercises.explain: The main purpose of an agent is to automatically search for information, making tasks easier for users.

exercises.prompt: Agents always find the best results for you without any input.
exercises.explain: Agents need clear queries from users to find useful results; they don't know what you want without guidance.

exercises.prompt: An agent helps by ________ information quickly from various sources.
exercises.sentence: An agent helps by extracting information quickly from various sources.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'extracting,' as it means pulling out specific information from the web.

exercises.prompt: Which statement is an example of a successful web task?

options.text: Searching for a movie and finding reviews.
Ignoring all the links on the results page.

exercises.explain: Searching for a movie and finding reviews shows a successful task because it involves typing a question and getting useful information.

exercises.prompt: When an agent searches the web, what are the essential steps it performs?

options.text: Search, ignore, and save the results.
Search, navigate, extract data, and submit a query.
Only search and then display ads.

exercises.explain: The correct steps are search, navigate, extract data, and submit a query, which is how an agent effectively performs web tasks.
